steps.title: Find the foreign-versus-local contrast
steps.content: The most telling detail is the explicit pairing of activism for “the exile from far-distant shores” with the call to “remember” that “men are still wasting life's crimson around your own doors.”

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
"When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores, / Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors." (from "An Appeal to My Countrywomen") is the speaker directly criticizing activists (“ye plead”) by reminding them that while they focus on distant exiles, people suffer at home—the exact hypocrisy described in the claim.

A, B, and C don't capture this selective attention to foreign causes over local ones.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
